sections.paragraph:
, once כֹּול ( Jer 33:8 כתיב), followed by Makkeph כָּל־ m. prop. subst. the whole, totality, das Ganze, die Gefammtheit, from the root כָּלַל to complete. (Arabic كُلُّ, Syriac ܟܽܠ id. [“Sam. , Ethiop. ኩል፡”]. To this answer the Greek ὅλος, Lat. ullus, comp. No. 4, Germ. all, alle, omnes, and heil, totus, Engl. all and whole.) In western languages it has to be rendered by adjectives.

(1) If used with regard to one continuous thing, the whole, totus, a, um (ὅλος, ganz); followed by a substantive (regarded as being in the genitive) either made definite by the article (like the Greek πᾶσα ἡ γῆ, toute la terre, die ganze Erde), or by a genitive of a noun or pronoun, unless it be a proper name, which needs no such definition: [in English this has to be expressed either by whole preceded by the article, or by all followed by it; when the noun is made definite by a pronoun suffixed, it must be rendered in English by all without the article, or else by the whole of]; כָּל־הָאָרֶץ the whole earth, all the earth, Gen 9:19 11:1 כָּל־הָעָס the whole people, Gen 19:4 כָּל־חַצּאֹן the whole flock, Gen 31:8 כָּל־הָאַיִל the whole ram, Exo 29:18 כָּל־הַיֹּום the whole day (see יֹום , letter , β ); כָּל־כִּכַּר הַיַּרְדֵּן the whole circuit of Jordan, Gen 13:10 כָּל־אֶרֶץ כּוּשׁ the whole land of Ethiopia, Gen 2:13 compare Gen 14:7 41:8 45:20 כָּל־עַמִּי all my people, Gen 41:40 בְּכָל לְבָֽבְךָ וּבְכָל נַפְשְׁךָ Deu 4:29 2Sa 9:9 Gen 2:2 כָּל־יִשְׂרָאֵל all the people of Israel, 1Ch 11:1. With suff. כֻּלָּךְ, בֻּלֵּךְ all of thee, Isa 14:29, 31 Isa 14:31 22:1 כֻּלֹּו all of him, Gen 25:25 כֹּל is rarely placed after in the genitive (in the same manner as the phrase הַר הַקֹּדֶשׁ and the like) as חָזוּת הַכֹּל the whole vision, Isa 29:10 more often with a suffix, as יִשְׂרָאֵל כֻּלֹּח prop. Israel, it the whole, 2Sa 2:9 מִצְרַיִם כֻּלָּהּ for כָּל־מִצְרַיִם Eze 29:2 תֵּבֵל כֻּלָּהּ Job 34:13. (As to a similar use of the Arabic words كُلُّ and جميع see De Sacy, Gramm. Arabe ii. § 68.)

(2) When it refers to many things, many individuals, all, omnes, omnia.-(a) followed by a plural, made definite (compare tous les hommes); כָּל־הַגֹּויִם all peoples, Isa 2:2 25:7 כָּל־הַלֵּילֹות all nights, Isa 21:8 כָּל־הָרְשָׁעִים all the wicked, Psa 145:20 כָּל־הַנֹּפְלִים all those who fall, Psa 145:14 כָּל־הַיָּמִים all days (i.e. in all time, always; see יֹום ); כָּל־יְמֵי אָדָם all the days of (the life of) Adam, Gen 5:5 כָּל־בְּנֵי לֵוִי all the Levites, Exo 32:25 כָּל־ישְׁבֵי תֵבֵל Isa 18:3 כָּל־מַלְכֵי גֹויִם Isa 14:9 כָּל־נִפְלְאֹותֶיךָ all thy wondrous works, Psa 9:2. But however, poetically, without art. כָּל־יָדַיִם Isa 13:7 כָּל־שֻׁלְתָנֹות Isa 28:8 compare Isa 51:18, 20 Isa 51:20. With pl. suff. כֻּלָּנוּ all of us, Gen 42:11 כֻּלְּכֶם all of you, Deu 1:22 כֻּלָּם all of them, they all, Isa 14:10, 17 Isa 14:17 31:3 f. כֻּלָּנָה Gen 42:36 כֻּלָּֽהְנָה 1Ki 7:37. Also followed by a relative, Gen 6:2, כָּל אֲשֶׁר בָּחָרוּ “all (the virgins) whom they chose;” Gen 7:22, כָּל־אֲשֶׁר נ׳ רוּחַ חַיִּים בְּאַפָּיז “all in whose nostrils was the breath of life;” Gen 39:5, כָּל־אֲשֶׁר יֵשׁ לֹו “whatsoever he had,” and ellipt. כָּל־הֲכִינֹותִי “all things (which) I have prepared,” 1Ch 29:3. Also followed by a periphr. בְּבָל־דֹּר וָדֹר in all ages, Ps. 45:18 , בְּכָל־יֹום וָיֹום Est 2:11.-(b) followed by a collective singular always having the article, as כָּל־הָאָדָם Gen 7:21 Jud. 16:17 כָּל־הַחַיָּה all animals, Gen 8:1 כֻּלֹּו they all, Isa 1:23 כָּל־זאֹת all this, Isa 5:25 (a demonstrative pronoun not requiring the article).-(c) followed by a singular without the article, it is, all, every one, whoever, whatever, omnis, omne; quivis, quodvis; Germ. jeder (French tout homme), e.g. בְּבָל־שָׁנָה every year, Est 9:21 כָּל־פֶּה every mouth, Isa 9:17 כָּל־בַּיִת every house, Isaiah 24:10 כָּל־פָּנִים every face, Isa 25:8 compare, Isa 15:2 24:11 30:25 40:4 45:24 כָּל־כָּבֹוד all splendour, every thing splendid, Isa 4:5 כָּל־גֵּאֶה whatever is high, Isa 2:12. Here also belongs כָּל־אָדָם every man, Job 21:33 37:7 Psa 39:6 and כָּל־בָּשָׂר all flesh, all that is of flesh, πᾶσα σὰρξ, all mortals. Gen 6:12, 13 Isa 40:5 49:26 .-(d) absol, it is put-(a) without the art. כֹּל omnes, all, Isa 30:5, כֹּל־חֹבִאישׁ they were all ashamed “all things (which are),” Isa 44:24.-(β) with the art. הַכֹּל all (men); Gen 16:12, יָדֹו בַכֹּל “his hand against all men;” Ecc 9:2, הַכֹּל כַּאֲשֶׁד לַכֹּל “to all it shall be, as to all,” the same lot awaits all; Job 24:24, הֻמְּכוּ כַּכֹּל יִקָּֽפְצוּן “they melt away, like all the rest they are carried away” (where כַּכֹּל is put for the fuller כְּבָל־הָאָדָם Jud. 16:17 ); also all things; Ecc 1:2, הַכֹּלהֶבֶל “all things are vanity;” Ecc 12:8, Dan 11:2.

Followed by a noun not made definite, it is also-(3) any, whosoever, ullus, quicunque; as כָּל־דָּבָר anything whatsoever (irgend eine Sache), Rth 4:7 Levit. 4:2 Num 35:22 Eze 15:3 hence with a negation, לֹא and אֵין, not any, no one, non ullus, nullus. 2Ch 32:15, לֹא יוּכַל כָּל־אֱלֹוהַּ כָּל גֹּוי … לְחַצִּיל עַמֹּו “nor is any god of any people able to save his people.” Exo 12:16, כָּל־מְלָאכָה לֹא יֵעָשֶׂה “not any work shall be done,” i.e. no work shall be done. Pro 12:21, יְאֻנֶּה לַצַּדִּיק כָּל־אָוֶן לֹא “there shall no evil happen to the righteous.” Ecc 1:9, אֵין כָּל־חָדָשׁ “there is not anything new.” Gen 3:1 Exo 10:15 20:4 Lev 3:17 Jud. 19:19 Pro 30:30. A difference must be made in the passage, Psa 49:18, לֹא בְּמֹותֹו יִקַּח הַכֹּל (where כֹּל has the article) “when he dies he does not take away all this,” im Tode nimmt er das Alles nicht mit fich; and also in those passages where כֹּל is followed by a defined substantive, and signifies the whole, totus. 1Sa 14:24 לֹא טָעַם כָּל־הָעָם לֶחֶם “the whole people did not taste food.” Num 13:23, כֻּלֹּו לֹא תִרְאֶה “the whole of him thou wilt not see (but only a part).” And-

(4) all, of all kinds, every sort, omnis generis, varius (like the Gr. πᾶς, for παντοῖος, παντοδαπός, Il. i. 5; and just as a periphrastic plural is used in speaking of things of many kinds, Biel for Bielerleh, Gram. § 106, 4). כָּל־עֵץ trees of every kind, Levit. 19:23 כָּל־מֶכֶר saleable commodities of every kind, Neh 13:16 1Ch 29:2.

(5) Adv. it is put for πάντως, all, wholly, altogether, omnino, plane, prorsus; placed before-(a) substantives, Psa 39:6, כָּל־הֶבֶל כָּל־אָרָם “altogether vanity is every man,” ganz eitel, lauter Gitelkeit, i.q. אַךְ הֶבֶל.-(b) other adverbs, especially in the later Hebrew. כָּל־עֻמַּת שֶׁ wholly as, altogether like (gerade fo mie, ebenfo wie), Ecc 5:15 כָּל־עֹוד altogether in the same period of time, eben fo lange, ganz fo lange, Job 27:3. Comp. Lehrg. p. 626.

Note. When כֹּל stands connected with a feminine substantive, or a plural, the predicate commonly agrees in gender and number with such noun as being the more important word, e.g. כָּל־הַנְּשָׁמָה תְהַלֵּל Psa 150:6 rarely with כֹּל as the governing word, Gen 9:29 Exo 12:16 Nah 3:7.-כֹּל is found separated from its genitive, Hos 14:3, כָּל־תִּשָּׂא עָוֹון.

כל

followed by Makk. כָּל־ Chald. i.q. heb-

(1) followed by a sing. whole, totus. כָּל־מַלְכוּתָא the whole kingdom, Ezr 6:11, 12 Ezr 6:12 7:16 .

(2) all, omnes, followed by a plur. Dan 3:2 5:7 . With suff. כָּלְּהֹון all of them, Dan 2:38 7:19 . Absol. in emphat. st. כֹּלָּא (Milêl, in the Syriac manner) i.q. Hebr. חַכֹּל all, omnia; (not adverbially, altogether, as it is made through some error by Winer, p. 481). Dan 2:40, חָשֵׁל כֹּלָּא “breaking to pieces all things;” Dan 4:9, מזֹון לְכֹלָּא בֵהּ “food for all (was) in it” (the tree). Dan 4:25 Ezr 5:7.

(3) any, whosoever, ullus, quicunque, Dan 6:8.

(4) adv. like Heb. No. 5, altogether; used redundantly prefixed to other adverbs, (in the Aramean manner, in which particles heaped upon one another weakly are so commonly used); in the phrases כָּל־קְבֵל־דְּנָה altogether on that account, כָּל־קְבֵל־דְּ wholly because, for the simple because (see קְבֵּל ).
